Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Intermediate A sophisticated yet intermediate-level attack targeting water/wastewater infrastructure begins with a phishing campaign exploiting a known vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server (CVE-2021-35394). The attackers leverage this initial foothold to deploy malware, establish persistence, escalate privileges, and ultimately manipulate SCADA systems to disrupt operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-35394 [CWE-200]: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: Attackers send spear-phishing emails containing malicious links or attachments to employees. When clicked, these exploit the Microsoft Exchange Server RCE vulnerability (CVE-2021-35394) to gain unauthorized access to the network through internet-facing mail servers.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-40444 [CWE-119]: Microsoft Office Memory Corruption Vulnerability
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 44: Overflow Binary Resource File
stage.description: Embedded macros in malicious .docm files trigger a buffer overflow exploit (CVE-2021-40444) to execute arbitrary code, deploying a lightweight backdoor onto the victim's system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created to periodically execute the backdoor, ensuring continued access even after initial entry points are closed or detected.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Known exploits like EternalBlue or unpatched SCADA system flaws are leveraged to elevate privileges from standard user accounts to administrative levels.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Payloads are encrypted/encoded using double encoding techniques to bypass signature-based detection, while logging mechanisms are disabled to cover tracks.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: Critical infrastructure controls (e.g., pump rates, chemical dosages) are altered to cause operational disruptions, mirroring SolarWinds-style indirect impacts through compromised systems.

Scenario.summary: This intermediate-level attack compromises water/wastewater systems through a phishing campaign exploiting Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities, escalating privileges to manipulate SCADA systems and cause operational disruption.
